task_completion.description: Open Panoply and load the air.mon.ltm.nc dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply 'Sources' window should be visible with 'air.mon.ltm.nc' loaded in the file list.

task_completion.description: Create a plot for the 'air' variable (surface air temperature) and set the time dimension to January.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a map of the 'air' variable, with the time/date array control explicitly set to the first time step (January).

task_completion.description: Export the temperature plot for the Tibetan Plateau as 'tibet_temp_jan.png' to the LithiumProspecting directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or Panoply save dialog showing 'tibet_temp_jan.png' located in the '~/Documents/LithiumProspecting/' directory.

task_completion.description: Export the temperature plot for the Andes as 'andes_temp_jan.png' to the LithiumProspecting directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or Panoply save dialog showing 'andes_temp_jan.png' located in the '~/Documents/LithiumProspecting/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create the evaporation feasibility analysis text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager showing 'evaporation_feasibility.txt' created in the '~/Documents/LithiumProspecting/' directory.

task_completion.description: Identify the correct Celsius temperatures without applying the misleading Kelvin conversion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The plot legends or the contents of 'evaporation_feasibility.txt' must show realistic Celsius values (e.g., Tibet < 0°C, Andes > 0°C, overall range roughly -62 to +39). They must NOT show extremely negative values (like -250°C) which would indicate the agent blindly subtracted 273.15 from data already in Celsius.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to visualize the data as instructed, rather than bypassing it with a Python script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Panoply application interface open and being interacted with.

integrity.description: The exported PNG files are genuine map plots generated by Panoply.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the contents of the PNG files are visible, they should be actual geographic data plots, not empty files, generic screenshots, or text.

integrity.description: The feasibility text file contains actual comparative analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file should contain a written comparison of the two regions' temperatures, not just a copy-paste of the task prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset 'air.mon.ltm.nc' contains the 'air' variable natively in degrees Celsius ('degC'). The task prompt includes a misleading instruction to convert from Kelvin to Celsius by subtracting 273.15. The agent must recognize the data is already in Celsius (visible in Panoply metadata) and ignore this instruction. Correct outputs will show realistic Celsius temperatures (Tibet < 0°C, Andes > 0°C). If the agent blindly applies the formula, values will be extremely negative (e.g., around -270°C), which is incorrect. Expected output files are 'tibet_temp_jan.png', 'andes_temp_jan.png', and 'evaporation_feasibility.txt' in ~/Documents/LithiumProspecting/.
